text.title: Twitter/X Positioning, Content Strategy, and Thread Structure
text.summary: Positioning formula, content pillar definitions, thread structure, and reply strategy for Twitter/X growth.

rule.statement: Define your Twitter identity across four elements before posting: topic (what you talk about), angle (what is unique), voice (how you sound), and format (content types).
rule.rationale: Without a clear positioning, followers cannot predict what they will get and have no reason to follow. Positioning creates expectation and repeat engagement.

rule.statement: Bio formula: [What you do] | Building [product] | [Social proof] | [CTA]. Keep under 160 characters.
rule.rationale: The bio is the first conversion surface. Visitors who land on your profile from a reply or mention will decide in 3 seconds whether to follow based on the bio.

rule.statement: Pinned tweet should be the best-performing content or a clear offer. Update it when a new high-performer emerges.
rule.rationale: The pinned tweet is the second thing visitors see after the bio. It should demonstrate content quality and provide a CTA.

rule.statement: Thread tweet 1 must standalone as a compelling post without reading the rest — it is the main distribution surface. External links reduce reach; put links in the first reply or bio.
rule.rationale: X's algorithm uses tweet 1 engagement (likes, retweets, bookmarks) to decide whether to distribute the rest of the thread. A weak tweet 1 kills the whole thread.

list.item: Tweet 1: Hook — problem or bold claim
Tweet 2: Promise — what they will learn

example.note: Tweet 1 for a build-in-public account: "I went from $0 to $15K MRR in 8 months without paid ads. Here's the exact playbook: [thread emoji]" — specific numbers, promise of value, clear format signal.

antipattern.description: Thread hook: "I have 10 tips for growing on Twitter [thread]"
antipattern.reason: Generic number-list hooks are overused. The algorithm sees low engagement on tweet 1 and does not distribute the thread. Use niche-specific, counterintuitive hooks instead.

section.title: Reply Strategy

rule.statement: Reply to 20-30 accounts larger than you daily using the [React] + [Add value] + [Question or take] formula. Be first or early with a valuable reply.
rule.rationale: Being a consistent, early, thoughtful voice in 3-5 large accounts' comments builds recognition faster than standalone posting because it borrows existing audience attention.

rule.statement: Build-in-public posts with real numbers (MRR, user count, specific percentages) outperform vague narrative posts by 2-5x on engagement.
rule.rationale: Specificity signals authenticity and provides concrete value. Audiences can benchmark their own progress against real numbers; they cannot benchmark against vague claims.

section.reference: 10-tweet thread structure with hook, promise, value, summary, and CTA slots
Agent prompt for 15 tweets + 1 thread + 1 build-in-public update per week
